meanings.definition: put in motion or move to act

examples: The family took off for Florida

meanings.definition: direct attention to, as if by means of contrast
examples: This dress accentuates your nice figure!
I set off these words by brackets

meanings.definition: cause to burst with a violent release of energy

examples: His skills offset his opponent's superior strength

meanings.definition: set in motion or cause to begin
examples: The guide set the tour off to a good start
set off great unrest among the people
